message:
fix: replace wall_now() with sleep_with_cx in production cancel-watchers

Three cancel-watcher loops used asupersync::time::wall_now() as the
timer origin instead of the cx's timer driver. Under LabRuntime this
prevents deterministic virtual time advancement (same root cause as
the labruntime_observation test regressions fixed in d28a7e61).

- web/server.rs: both unix and non-unix shutdown watchers now use
  sleep_with_cx(cx, 100ms) instead of budget_sleep(cx, 100ms, wall_now())
- distributed.rs: race_with_cx_cancel watcher now uses sleep_with_cx(cx, 50ms)
  instead of asupersync::time::sleep(wall_now(), 50ms), gaining budget
  awareness and proper cx-timer integration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
